concerns.text: Constitution Principle VI mandates that any reported correlation between cosmic‑ray anisotropy and solar activity be demonstrated **independently** in both IceCube and Pierre Auger datasets (each must reach ≥3σ). The Success Criteria (SC‑004, SC‑005) only require a single solar proxy to yield |r|≥0.4 with p≤0.01, and do not enforce per‑detector significance. This directly violates the Multi‑Detector Consistency rule, making the validation plan scientifically unsound.

The block‑bootstrap significance test uses a block length of 27 days, yet the hypothesis concerns an ∼11‑year modulation. Such short blocks cannot preserve the long‑range autocorrelation structure of the dipole‑amplitude series, risking severe under‑estimation of variance and inflated significance. The methodology therefore does not reliably test the claimed solar‑cycle correlation.
